steps: a. Read row token_emb[token_id] (dequantize if needed)

Output is always fp32 for downstream computation

constraints.notes: Q5_0 requires K dimension multiple of 256 for dequantization. Produces head-major output for efficient attention.

inputs.name: bias
inputs.desc: Bias vector [N] (or NULL for no bias)

outputs.name: C
outputs.desc: Output matrix [M, N] = A @ B + bias

strategies.name: output
strategies.notes: Split by output rows; each thread computes subset of C.

strategies.name: feature
strategies.notes: Split by output features; each thread computes subset of output columns.

constraints.notes: GEMM nt: C = A @ B^T. B is stored as [N, K] row-major. K and N should be multiples of 64.

kernels.notes: General matrix multiplication: C = A @ B^T + bias. This is the 'nt' variant; weights are stored as [N, K]. Used for projection layers in transformer blocks.
kernels.name: gemm_blocked_serial

constraints.notes: Q4_K uses 256-value super-blocks; K should be a multiple of 256.

impl.c_declaration: void gemm_nt_q4_k(const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K);

kernels.notes: GEMM for Q4_K weights with FP32 activation input. No activation quantization needed.
kernels.name: gemm_nt_q4_k

strategies.name: token
strategies.notes: Split by rows (tokens); good for prefill with many tokens.

strategies.name: feature
strategies.notes: Split by output features; good for decode.

constraints.notes: Q4_K requires K dimension multiple of 256 for dequantization. Most aggressive quantization for W2 (down) projection.

parity.notes: Compare against numpy matmul (higher tolerance due to quantization)

kernels.notes: General matrix multiplication with Q4_K weights and Q8_K activations. Used for W2 (down) projection in MLP. High compression ratio.
kernels.name: gemm_nt_q4_k_q8_k

constraints.notes: Q5_0 uses 32-value blocks; K should be a multiple of 32.

impl.c_declaration: void gemm_nt_q5_0(const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K);

kernels.notes: GEMM for Q5_0 weights with FP32 activation input. No activation quantization needed.
kernels.name: gemm_nt_q5_0

strategies.name: token
strategies.notes: Split by rows (tokens); good for prefill with many tokens.

strategies.name: feature
strategies.notes: Split by output features; good for decode.

constraints.notes: Q5_0 requires K dimension multiple of 256 for dequantization. B is transposed (nt = A * B^T).

kernels.notes: General matrix multiplication with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 activations. Used for QKV projection and output projection in prefill.
kernels.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_q8_0

constraints.notes: Q6_K uses 256-value super-blocks; K should be a multiple of 256.

strategies.name: feature
strategies.notes: Split by output features; good for decode.

constraints.notes: Q6_K requires K dimension multiple of 256 for dequantization. Alternative to Q4_K for W2 (down) projection with better accuracy.

kernels.notes: General matrix multiplication with Q6_K weights and Q8_K activations. Used for W2 (down) projection in MLP when Q4_K accuracy is insufficient.

constraints.notes: Q8_0 uses 32-value blocks; K should be a multiple of 32.

impl.c_declaration: void mega_fused_attention_prefill(float *output, const float *input, const float *residual, const float *ln1_gamma, const void *wq, const float *bq, CKDataType wq_dt, const void *wk, const float *bk, CKDataType wk_dt, const void *wv, const float *bv, CKDataType wv_dt, const void *wo, const float *bo, CKDataType wo_dt, float *kv_cache_k, float *kv_cache_v, const float *rope_cos, const float *rope_sin, int start_pos, int tokens, int cache_capacity, int embed_dim, int aligned_embed_dim, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, float eps, void *scratch);

kernels.notes: This is the 'holy grail' fusion - RMSNorm+QKV+RoPE+Attention+OutProj+Residual in single DRAM round-trip

parity.notes: Compare fused MLP output against unfused reference

kernels.notes: Mega-fused OutProj + MLP: Quantize attn_out → OutProj → Residual → RMSNorm → MLP (w1*w3→SwiGLU→w2) → Residual. Fuses 8 operations into 1.
kernels.name: mega_fused_outproj_mlp_prefill

impl.c_declaration: void rmsnorm_forward(const float *input, const float *gamma, float *output, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps);

kernels.notes: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization. Computes: output = input / sqrt(mean(x^2) + eps) * gamma
kernels.name: rmsnorm_forward

inputs.name: q
inputs.desc: Query tensor [heads, tokens, head_dim] to be rotated in-place

inputs.name: k
inputs.desc: Key tensor [kv_heads, tokens, head_dim] to be rotated in-place

params.name: pos_offset
params.desc: Position offset for cos/sin cache lookup

strategies.name: head
strategies.notes: Parallelize across heads; each thread handles one or more heads.

strategies.name: token
strategies.notes: Parallelize across tokens; each thread handles one or more tokens.

impl.c_declaration: void rope_forward_qk(float *q, float *k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset);

parity.notes: Verify RoPE rotation matches numpy reference

kernels.notes: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) for queries and keys. Applies rotation in complex plane: x_rot = x * cos(theta) + rotate_half(x) * sin(theta)
kernels.name: rope_forward_qk

inputs.name: gate
inputs.desc: Gate tensor [tokens, intermediate_dim] (first half of MLP intermediate)

inputs.name: up
inputs.desc: Up tensor [tokens, intermediate_dim] (second half of MLP intermediate)

scratch.name: gate_activated
scratch.desc: SiLU(gate) activation (can reuse output if careful)

strategies.name: token
strategies.notes: Each thread processes one or more complete tokens.

strategies.name: feature
strategies.notes: Parallelize across intermediate dimension; requires sigmoid and multiplication.

constraints.notes: SiLU(x) = x * sigmoid(x). I should be multiple of 64 for optimal SIMD.

impl.c_declaration: void swiglu_forward(const float *input, float *output, int tokens, int dim);

parity.notes: Verify SiLU(x) * up matches numpy reference

kernels.notes: SwiGLU activation: SiLU(gate) * up = gate * sigmoid(gate) * up. Used in LLaMA-style MLP blocks.
kernels.name: swiglu_forward

kernels.description: BPE tokenizer using trie data structure for UTF-8 text to token ID conversion

encode_params.name: text_len
encode_params.description: Length of input text in bytes (-1 for strlen)

vocab_offsets.description: Offset into vocab_strings for each token

steps: 1. Build trie from vocab_offsets + vocab_strings (or use prebuilt)
a. Walk trie to find longest matching token
b. If no match, apply BPE merges or use <unk>

implementation_notes: Trie can be prebuilt at model load time from vocab_offsets/vocab_strings
BPE merges needed for handling out-of-vocabulary subwords
Special tokens: <pad>, <unk>, <bos>, <eos> typically at fixed IDs
UTF-8 aware: must handle multi-byte characters correctly
kernels.name: tokenizer_bpe_trie
